seoTitle: How to Add a Clickable Website Link Button in Power BI
metaDescription: Learn how to add a clickable button in Power BI that opens a website link, so dashboard viewers can jump straight to docs or external resources in one click.
intro: Power BI dashboards get a lot more useful when readers can click straight through to a related website instead of hunting for a link elsewhere. This walkthrough shows how to turn a shape or button into a working hyperlink to any web page. By the end you'll be able to point users to documentation, source data, or company pages directly from your report.

steps.heading: Decide what the button should link to
steps.body: Before adding anything to the canvas, settle on the destination URL and where on the report the button makes sense, such as near a header or a related visual.

steps.heading: Insert a button or shape
steps.body: Add a button or shape from the Insert ribbon in Power BI Desktop, then resize and position it where you want the clickable element to sit.

steps.heading: Set the action to a web URL
steps.body: Turn on the Action setting for the button, choose Web URL as the type, and paste in the address you want it to open.

steps.heading: Style it so it reads as clickable
steps.body: Adjust fill, text, and hover states so the button visually signals it can be clicked, which helps viewers who aren't used to interactive reports.

steps.heading: Test in reading view and publish
steps.body: Switch out of edit mode to confirm the link opens correctly, then publish the report so the button works for everyone viewing it.

faq.question: Does the clickable button work in the Power BI mobile app?
faq.answer: Yes, buttons with a Web URL action work the same way on mobile as they do in the browser, opening the link in the device's default browser.

faq.question: Can I use an image instead of a shape as the button?
faq.answer: Yes, images support the same Action settings as shapes and buttons, so you can use a logo or icon as the clickable element instead.

faq.question: Will the link open in a new tab?
faq.answer: In most cases the link opens in a new browser tab or window rather than navigating away from the report, though behavior can vary slightly by platform.

faq.question: Can I link to another page within the same report instead of a website?
faq.answer: Yes, the same Action feature also supports linking to another report page, a bookmark, or a Q&A page in addition to external web URLs.
